info.name: Petstore API - Complete Collection
info.description: Complete Postman collection for Swagger Petstore API (https://petstore.swagger.io/v2). Covers all 14 endpoints across Pet, Store, and User resources with test scripts, pre-request scripts, and all success/error responses. No environment setup needed - base URL is hardcoded.

exec: // Generate a unique pet ID to avoid conflicts

request.description: Add a new pet to the store. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth.
Update an existing pet. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth.

exec: pm.test('Each pet has id and name', function () {
pm.test('Each pet status is available', function () {
// Save first available pet id for later use

query.description: Filter by status: available, pending, sold

request.description: Finds pets by status. Multiple status values can be provided with comma separated strings.
Find all pets with pending status.
Find all pets with sold status.
Finds pets by tags. Multiple tags can be provided. Deprecated - use findByStatus instead.
Returns a single pet by ID. Use api_key header 'special-key' for authorization.
Updates a pet in the store using form data. Replace '1' in the URL with the actual pet ID.

header.description: API key required to delete a pet

request.description: Deletes a pet by ID. Replace '9999999' with the actual pet ID. api_key header is required.
Uploads an image for a pet. Replace '1' with actual pet ID. Select a file in the form-data body.

exec: pm.test('Response has at least one status key', function () {

request.description: Returns a map of status codes to quantities. Requires api_key authorization.

item.name: Place an order for a pet

request.description: Place an order for a pet. Status can be: placed, approved, delivered.
Find purchase order by ID. Valid IDs are integers between 1 and 10.
Delete purchase order by ID. Valid IDs are positive integers.
Create a new user. This can only be done by the logged in user.
Creates list of users with given input array.
Creates list of users with given input list.

exec: pm.test('Response is a string (session token)', function () {

request.description: Logs user into the system. Returns a session token string.
Logs out the currently logged-in user session.
Get user by username. Replace 'john_doe_test' with the desired username.
Update user. Replace 'john_doe_test' in the URL with the username to update. Logged in user only.
Delete user. Replace 'john_doe_test' in the URL with the username to delete. Logged in user only.

variable.description: Stores the ID of a newly created pet (auto-set by Add a new pet request)
Stores the first available pet ID from findByStatus (auto-set)
Stores the ID of a newly placed order (auto-set by Place an order request)
